checks.name: schema_completeness
checks.detail: All required payload fields present including customSections for classification engine, digest format, and Notion schema

checks.name: flow_integrity
checks.detail: 4 flows (capture routing 15 edges, daily digest 9 edges, error recovery 7 edges) — all properly connected with error edges

checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: Polling every 30s for Telegram messages, daily 9PM digest schedule — both valid

checks.name: connector_role_alignment
checks.detail: telegram (chat_messaging), notion (knowledge_base), airtable (knowledge_base) — all builtins available

checks.name: adoption_question_fitness
checks.detail: 7 adoption_questions added covering Telegram chat ID, 3 Notion database IDs, Airtable base/table, and digest time

checks.name: default_value_coverage
checks.detail: Airtable table name (Tasks) and digest time (21:00) have defaults. Telegram/Notion/Airtable IDs require user input.

checks.name: prompt_quality
checks.detail: Identity 52w, instructions 600w+ with core loop and daily digest, tool guidance with Telegram/Notion/Airtable API endpoints, 4 examples (task/bookmark/idea/digest), comprehensive error handling

checks.name: safety_scan
checks.detail: 0 critical findings. Offset-based deduplication prevents reprocessing. Classification fallback to 'note' is safe default.

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity 52w (borderline but passes >50), instructions cover core loop with 5 steps and daily digest totaling 600+ words with classification heuristics, tool guidance with Telegram/Notion/Airtable API endpoints, 4 examples covering all capture types plus digest, error handling per service.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request for all 3 APIs with specific endpoint documentation. file_read/file_write for local state and offset tracking. All tools match suggested_tools.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: 30-second polling for near-real-time Telegram capture, daily 9PM digest. Both align with the described workflows. The 30s interval is aggressive but appropriate for personal capture.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: All 3 connectors (Telegram, Notion, Airtable) have builtins. Credential fields are comprehensive — Notion connector includes 3 separate database IDs for Ideas/Bookmarks/Notes.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Daily capture tracking via local file, offset persistence for Telegram deduplication, agent_memory for digest history and trend analysis. Good but could benefit from capture pattern learning.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Per-service handling: Telegram (401, 409, 429, timeout), Notion (400, 404, 429 with 350ms spacing), Airtable (422, 403). Classification fallback to 'note' with 'needs-review' tag. Failed captures saved to fallback file.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: 4 flows cover the complete lifecycle: capture routing with classification, daily digest compilation, and error recovery. All prompt steps map to flow nodes.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Clear value: single Telegram bot as the entry point for all captures, automatically classified and routed to the right destination. Replaces manual copy-paste across 3 apps.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: All connectors have builtins. Telegram BotFather setup is straightforward. Notion requires 3 separate databases with specific schemas. Airtable requires matching table fields. Multi-destination setup adds complexity.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: AI-powered message classification with priority-based heuristic pipeline, multi-destination routing, and structured metadata extraction (titles, tags, dates, priorities) go beyond simple forwarding.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: All 7 adoption questions are genuinely needed. 3 Notion DB IDs, Airtable base/table, Telegram chat ID, and digest time are all required for the agent to route captures correctly.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Most questions require user input (IDs with no possible defaults). Only Airtable table name and digest time have useful defaults. User must configure 5 of 7 fields before the template works.

issues: No adoption_questions existed — connectors need user-specific configuration for 3 Notion databases, Airtable base, and Telegram chat ID
7 required configuration fields is a high adoption barrier — consider a setup wizard or auto-discovery for Notion databases
30-second polling interval may be excessive for a personal capture tool — consider making it configurable

template: Added 7 adoption_questions: telegram_chat_id, notion_ideas_db, notion_bookmarks_db, notion_notes_db, airtable_base_id, airtable_table_name, digest_time
